repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue entry gives signed ego-cluster incidence-polynomial first and joint exposure probabilities.

published_prior_art.cite: AronowSamii2017 gives exposure-mapping HT probabilities abstractly and UganderKarrerBackstromKleinberg2013 gives exact probabilities for selected graph-cluster exposure conditions, but neither states the signed-polynomial inclusion-exclusion algebra.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue Experimentation/Stat entry derives an ego-overlap dependency graph for HT exposure contrasts.

published_prior_art.cite: Chin2018 assumes a restricted dependency graph for HT CLTs; this proposition derives a sufficient degree bound from ego support overlap.

repo_prior_art.cite: The closest repo discussion is this qid's prior review and gaps file; no accepted catalogue item contains the fixed-potential ego-cluster HT oracle Wald theorem.

published_prior_art.cite: AronowSamii2017 proves exposure-mapping HT asymptotic normality under local dependence and Chin2018 proves dependency-degree Stein CLTs; the new part is the ego-incidence primitive translation, not a new CLT frontier.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue entry gives a sharp conservative design-based variance envelope for nonadditive ego-cluster HT exposure contrasts.

published_prior_art.cite: AronowSamii2017, ChattopadhyayImaiZubizarreta2023, KungSussman2022, Leung2019ANI, and GaoDing2023 are adjacent variance/inference anchors but do not solve the stated sharp nonadditive ego-cluster envelope.

repo_prior_art.cite: Standard sanity reduction to local-dependence exposure-map benchmarks.

published_prior_art.cite: AronowSamii2017 and Chin2018 already cover the bounded-degree dependency-graph HT benchmark.

soundness_flags.label: oeq:conservative-variance
soundness_flags.one_line: The OEQ asks for asymptotic Wald coverage uniformly over def:ego-incidence-array-class, but that class omits the primitive degree-growth and variance-nondegeneracy restrictions needed by thm:ego-overlap-clt, so the target class is broader than the theorem it consumes.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:ego-overlap-clt
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The Wald interval is oracle because it uses sigma_n rather than an observed variance estimator.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The theorem is only an oracle design-based CLT/coverage benchmark under the stated primitive overlap, positivity, boundedness, and nondegenerate-variance conditions.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:conservative-variance
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The computable conservative variance envelope is explicitly open and no operational non-oracle CI is claimed.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Non-oracle coverage is only a solve target until Vbar_n and its sharpness/nontriviality properties are constructed.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Restrict oeq:conservative-variance to the CLT-qualified subclass, or explicitly replace the Wald-normal route with a finite-sample conservative inequality and a nontrivial minimality/sharpness requirement.

Make the variance-envelope handle a concrete finite-dimensional optimization problem: list variables, observed-outcome constraints, bounded-potential constraints, exact pi/pi_ij inputs, and whether Vbar_n is required to be minimal among valid envelopes.
Add a corollary that recovers the Aronow-Samii/Chin local-dependence benchmark when exposure events are singleton literals and then states exactly which ego-polynomial cases are newly covered.

Add LiuHuZhang2026 and the newer ego-cluster design papers as application-side comparators only if the prose keeps the distinction between model-based ego-cluster inference and fixed-potential design-based HT inference explicit.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle: the kernel is field-tier and novel enough, but the conservative-variance OEQ must be retargeted to the correct asymptotic class before Stage 0.
